findings.recommendation: Credentials must not be stored in the Git code or repository. There are ‘Secrets Management’ solutions that can be used to store secrets or use Pipeline resources.

findings.title: A hash algorithm used is considered weak and can cause hash collisions
findings.description: A hash algorithm used is considered weak and can cause hash collisions.
findings.recommendation: It is always recommended to use some CHF (Cryptographic Hash Function), which is mathematically strong and not reversible. SHA512 would be the most recommended hash for storing the password and it is also important to adopt some type of Salt, so that the Hash is more secure.

findings.title: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.Elem
findings.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.render-template-string.render-template-string
findings.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.
cwe_ids: CWE-96: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')
findings.code_excerpt:
return render_template_string("""

Welcome, {{ username }}!

Logout

""", username=username)

Welcome, {{ username }}!

Logout

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: javascript.lang.security.audit.md5-used-as-password.md5-used-as-password
findings.description: It looks like MD5 is used as a password hash. MD5 is not considered a secure password hash because it can be cracked by an attacker in a short amount of time. Use a suitable password hashing function such as bcrypt. You can use the `bcrypt` node.js package.
cwe_ids: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.xss.direct-response-write.direct-response-write
findings.description: Detected directly writing to a Response object from user-defined input. This bypasses any HTML escaping and may expose your application to a Cross-Site-scripting (XSS) vulnerability. Instead, use 'resp.render()' to render safely escaped HTML.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
findings.code_excerpt: res.send(`Welcome, ${username}! You are now logged in.`);

$ARG.abstract_content: `Welcome, username! You are now logged in.
``Welcome, username! You are now logged in.`

tool_specific.lines: res.send(`Welcome, ${username}! You are now logged in.`);

findings.title: trailofbits.python.tarfile-extractall-traversal.tarfile-extractall-traversal
findings.description: Possible path traversal through `tarfile.open($PATH).extractall()` if the source tar is controlled by an attacker
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-dill
findings.description: Avoid using `dill`, which uses `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
A08:2025 - Software or Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle
findings.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
A08:2025 - Software or Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: python.jwt.security.unverified-jwt-decode.unverified-jwt-decode
findings.description: Detected JWT token decoded with 'verify=False'. This bypasses any integrity checks for the token which means the token could be tampered with by malicious actors. Ensure that the JWT token is verified.

findings.title: python.jwt.security.jwt-hardcode.jwt-python-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: Hardcoded JWT secret or private key is used. This is a Insufficiently Protected Credentials weakness: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/522.html Consider using an appropriate security mechanism to protect the credentials (e.g. keeping secrets in environment variables)

findings.title: python.flask.security.open-redirect.open-redirect
findings.description: Data from request is passed to redirect(). This is an open redirect and could be exploited. Consider using 'url_for()' to generate links to known locations. If you must use a URL to unknown pages, consider using 'urlparse()' or similar and checking if the 'netloc' property is the same as your site's host name. See the references for more information.
cwe_ids: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

findings.title: python.boto3.security.hardcoded-token.hardcoded-token
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: javascript.lang.security.audit.path-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal
findings.description: Detected possible user input going into a `path.join` or `path.resolve` function. This could possibly lead to a path traversal vulnerability,  where the attacker can access arbitrary files stored in the file system. Instead, be sure to sanitize or validate user input first.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-libxml-vm-noent.express-libxml-vm-noent
findings.description: Detected use of parseXml() function with the `noent` field set to `true`. This can lead to an XML External Entities (XXE) attack if untrusted data is passed into it.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-audit
findings.description: Detected subprocess function 'call' without a static string. If this data can be controlled by a malicious actor, it may be an instance of command injection. Audit the use of this call to ensure it is not controllable by an external resource. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
